seoTitle: How to Create a Workspace in Power BI Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to create a collaborative Power BI workspace, choose a license capacity, and assign member roles for team access.
intro: Organizing enterprise reports and separating sandbox testing from production data starts with setting up a proper workspace. This guide shows how to create a new workspace in Power BI Service, choose the right license capacity, and assign member roles like Admin, Contributor, or Viewer. It's aimed at team leads setting up a shared reporting hub for the first time.

steps.heading: Open the Workspaces panel
steps.body: Sign into Power BI Service and click the Workspaces folder icon in the left sidebar.

steps.heading: Start a new workspace
steps.body: Click New workspace at the bottom of the navigation drawer, then type a distinctive name and an optional description.

steps.heading: Choose license capacity
steps.body: Expand Advanced settings and use the License capacity dropdown to pick between Fabric, Premium, or standard Pro workspaces.

steps.heading: Apply and create
steps.body: Customize brand colors if desired, then click Apply to generate your fresh workspace.

steps.heading: Assign access and organize
steps.body: Click Manage access to invite team members with specific roles, and use New folder to organize content for different sub-teams.

faq.question: What roles can I assign to workspace members?
faq.answer: You can assign Admin, Member, Contributor, or Viewer roles, each with different levels of access to content and management.

faq.question: What's the difference between Pro and Premium capacity?
faq.answer: Pro licenses require individual user licensing, while Premium Per User or Fabric F-SKU capacities offer expanded features and scaling for larger teams.

faq.question: Can I organize content into folders inside a workspace?
faq.answer: Yes, the 2026 workspace interface supports sub-folder structures to organize semantic models, reports, and dashboards.

faq.question: How do I test that my new workspace is working?
faq.answer: Click Upload to push a local report file into the workspace and confirm it loads correctly for your team.
